componentDescription: Topbar is the horizontal navigation bar fixed at the top of the application. Use it as the primary chrome for branding, global navigation, search, and user controls. Typically used alongside a Sidebar for full application layouts.
features: Left, center, and right content slots
compositionPattern: Place the application logo in the logo prop. Use leftSlot for secondary navigation or breadcrumbs. Use centerSlot for a global search bar. Use rightSlot for user avatar menu, notifications, and settings.

usageExamples.title: Standard app topbar
usageExamples.description: Topbar with logo, search, and user controls.

} centerSlot={ } rightSlot={
} sticky />

usageExamples.title: Topbar with breadcrumb navigation
usageExamples.description: Topbar with breadcrumbs in the left slot for context.
usageExamples.code:
<Topbar
  logo={<AppLogo />}
  leftSlot={
    <Breadcrumb
      items={[
        { label: 'Projects', onClick: () => navigate('/projects') },
        { label: currentProject.name, onClick: () => navigate(`/projects/${currentProject.id}`) },
        { label: 'Settings' },
      ]}
    />
  }
  rightSlot={<UserMenu user={currentUser} />}
  sticky
/>

logo.llmContext: React node for the application or brand logo on the left side. Should be compact — typically 32-40px tall. Make it a link to the home page for conventional navigation.
logo.propDescription: Logo or brand element on the left side

leftSlot.llmContext: React node rendered next to the logo on the left. Use for secondary navigation like breadcrumbs, page title, or environment selector (Production / Staging).
leftSlot.propDescription: Content rendered in the left section of the topbar

centerSlot.llmContext: React node rendered in the horizontal center of the topbar. Best used for a global search input — it provides prominent access to search from any page.
centerSlot.propDescription: Content rendered in the center of the topbar

rightSlot.llmContext: React node rendered on the far right. Use for user account controls — notification bell, help link, settings icon, and user avatar menu. Keep this area compact.
rightSlot.propDescription: Content rendered in the right section of the topbar

sticky.llmContext: Set to true to keep the topbar fixed at the top of the viewport when the page scrolls. Use for application-level topbars that should always be accessible. If using sticky, ensure stickyHeader on Tabs uses the correct offsetTop.
sticky.propDescription: Fixes the topbar to the top of the viewport on scroll
